name: Opera Creams <French Creams>
recipeInstructions: 2 tbsp corn syrup for softer candy. Combine sugar, cream and cream of tartar in a 3-quart heavy saucepan. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves. Continue cooking to 236°F (soft ball stage). Watch carefully to avoid boiling over. If sugar crystals form on the side of the pan, wipe them off. To prevent this problem, I place the lid on just as my candies start to boil for about 1 minute, which automatically washes the crystals down. Pour out onto a marble slab and let stand until lukewarm (about 5-10 minutes). Work like fondant until creamy and light in color. Add vanilla and work it into the mixture. Shape into pieces as desired (ovals, rounds, squares, etc.). Dip in melted chocolate. Makes 3 1/2 lbs. Note: The candy may be chilled in the freezer before dipping for best results. Good if dipped on the same day. Source: Betty Amrein, a great candy maker. Dolores, Hamilton Ohio 04/29 06:36 pm
